title: ranunculus pygmaeus subsp. sabinei
intro: Ranunculus pygmaeus subsp. sabinei, also known as Sabine's buttercup, is a rare alpine plant species belonging to the Ranunculaceae family. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Sabine's buttercup include its ability to relieve symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes, although its use should be under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
